hypothesis: Fine-tuning pre-trained ML interatomic potentials (such as eSEN or eqV2) specifically on stress tensor data generated along DFT-converged SSNEB pathways—rather than on bulk equilibrium configurations—will disproportionately reduce stress prediction errors and eliminate the symmetry-breaking artifacts currently observed during VASP restarts, thereby enabling fully ML-driven SSNEB convergence (without any DFT restart) that matches DFT accuracy to within chemical precision for energy barriers across polymorphic phase transitions in soft-lattice materials like halide perovskites.
